report_markdown:
## Week of May 25 — The Empty Calendar Lie

Your calendar showed 0 meeting hours this week. Your task list shows 0 completions. By those two numbers, this was your lightest week in months. It wasn't.

You spent $3,142, received 125 emails, flew 6.8 hours across two connections to move to a new city, opened 5 new bank accounts, and got rejected by Blue Origin — all while your Todoist sat untouched since April 13.

**The inbox is your shadow task system.** Everything that actually needed doing this week lived in your email: correcting the address on your State Farm renters insurance (at 12:31 AM), escalating the FedEx luggage pickup that failed, linking Ally to Amex to Fidelity to Forbright in a chain of Plaid connections, tracking two simultaneous June 1 deadlines — old rent ending, new apartment starting. None of it was a task. All of it was cognitive load.

**The financial account sprint is a flag.** Opening 5 accounts in 7 days — Ally, Amex Rewards Checking, Marcus by Goldman Sachs, Forbright, Fidelity — is not financial organization. It's financial anxiety expressed as action. You're about to start a new chapter in a new city and you're hedging everything simultaneously. The $3,101 withdrawal from Chase on May 29 likely makes sense in isolation. The pattern around it doesn't.

**Your task tool is 7 weeks old and still has the onboarding tutorials in it.** You have a BestEx Research recruiter who emailed you on May 31. You have a Blue Origin rejection that deserves 20 minutes of honest reflection. You have a new city, an empty apartment, and no system for any of it. These things aren't going to sort themselves through your inbox.

The root cause: You're running a full life transition entirely reactively — every commitment, deadline, and decision filtered through 125 emails a week instead of a single honest look at what actually needs to happen next.
root_cause: Every real obligation this week lived in your inbox, not a task system — which is why 0 tasks were completed while $3,142 moved and 125 emails landed.
